title: Functions
description: Bundle behavior into reusable functions with parameters and return values.
objectives: Accept parameters and return a value
Call a function by name and use what it returns
recap: Functions wrap a chunk of behavior behind a name. Parameters let callers pass different inputs; `return` hands a value back. `console.log` prints, `return` delivers — they're not the same thing.
practicePrompts: Write a function that squares a number and returns the result.
Write a function that returns the larger of two numbers.

practiceExercises.title: Square a number
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `square` that takes a number and returns it multiplied by itself. `square(4)` should return `16`.
practiceExercises.goal: Practice declaring and returning from a function.
hints: `return n * n;` inside the function body.

practiceExercises.title: Max of two numbers
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `maxOfTwo` that takes two numbers and returns the larger one. If they are equal, return either.
practiceExercises.goal: Use an if/else inside a function to choose what to return.
hints: `if (a > b) return a; return b;` — or the one-liner `return a > b ? a : b;`

practiceExercises.title: Even number check
practiceExercises.prompt: Write a function `isEven` that takes a number and returns `true` if it is even, `false` otherwise.
practiceExercises.goal: Return a boolean from a function using the modulo operator.
hints: The remainder after dividing by 2 tells you the answer.
`n % 2` is 0 for evens, non-zero for odds.
